tagline: Runs gross-to-net, withholds and funds pay — a payroll manager (CPP) signs the run before money moves.
problem: Payroll is high-volume gross-to-net under FLSA wage law plus trust-fund-tax withholding, where the money movement (ACH funding + tax deposit) is irreversible and unremitted withheld taxes carry personal liability under IRC 6672.
outcome: Hours computed to net, withholdings and FLSA/garnishment checks run straight-through; the payroll manager signs the run before net pay is funded by ACH and the 941 deposit is filed.

steps.does: Intake the timesheets / hours (worked, overtime, PTO)
Compute gross-to-net and the payroll tax (withholdings, employer taxes)
Screen FLSA minimum-wage / overtime, garnishment caps and classification
A payroll manager (CPP) approves the run before funds move
Fund net pay via ACH and file the tax deposit / Form 941
Reconcile the run and monitor the deposit / 941 status
